resources.string: vFlow service can perform various tasks through automated workflows.
vFlow service can perform various tasks through automated workflows. (Accessibility disguise enabled)
Automatically allow tool actions in this chat.
Run the built-in benchmark suite and score the current agent
Use the currently selected preset to run the fixed benchmark suite.
Open the panel to inspect model, accessibility, and benchmark host readiness.
Delete this benchmark run? This cannot be undone.
What would you like me to do for you?
Help me build a vFlow workflow that opens WeChat and taps the check-in button, and explain which modules it would use.
I want to build an automation task. Do not write the workflow yet. First help me figure out which vFlow modules, triggers, and logic branches I should combine.
Help me debug why a vFlow workflow is not triggering. Please give me a checklist covering trigger setup, permissions, conditions, and execution steps.
Please turn my natural-language automation request into an executable vFlow step plan, and point out which conditions I still need to provide.
This build currently sends text only. Attachments are not passed to the model yet.
Jump between previous chats or start with a clean context.
Delete conversation \"%s\"? This cannot be undone.
This model did not return separate thinking content.
Approve or reject the current tool request first.
Generate the app appearance from your wallpaper colors.
Enable colorful styling for workflow cards.
Replace the main bottom navigation with a Liquid Glass floating bar.
App restart needed to apply the new appearance settings
Show workflow execution status in the status bar.
Experimental feature. Pulling down Quick Settings can trigger recovery; if Shizuku is available, it adds extra process guarding. May increase battery usage.
Configure runtime parameters for various modules.
Manage persistent variables accessible from all workflows.
Manage providers, keys, and models used by chat.
Automatically check for new versions when opening Settings.
Allow dialogs and similar surfaces to appear on the lock screen.
Allow workflow access and control through LAN
Add a provider first, then continue with models and keys.
This only removes the model from the current provider.
Deleting a provider also removes its linked models.
Add one model first, then this provider can be used directly in chat.
When enabled, all app logs will be recorded in memory until the app is force stopped. Slightly increases memory usage.
Help diagnose crashes and performance issues
When enabled, anonymous device info, crash reports, and performance metrics are collected for troubleshooting. Data is processed by the Umeng SDK.
Scroll to the bottom to continue

If telemetry is enabled, the following anonymous data will be collected for troubleshooting:

• Device information (model, OS version, etc.)
• App crash reports
• Performance metrics

Data is processed by the Umeng SDK. You can turn this off later in Settings at any time.
Telemetry disabled, takes effect on next launch
Export the in-memory debug log buffer to a file.
Clear the debug logs recorded in the current session.
Run shell and permission diagnostics and display the result.
Test key listening and key event capture.
Manage vFlow Core installation, startup, and status.
Launch the floating UI inspector to inspect view hierarchy.
View and manage all permissions required by the app.
View app version, open source licenses, and developer info.
Disguise as a system accessibility service.
Please re-enable the accessibility service after toggling
To run workflow \"%s\" properly, please grant the following permissions:
Manage all permissions required by the app:
Tap a tile position. Assigned tiles will show the workflow name.
Are you sure you want to delete workflow \"%s\"? This action cannot be undone.
Do you want to save changes before exiting?

%1$d steps in this workflow still contain %2$d enum parameters stored as legacy display values. Do you want to migrate them to new version now?\n\nThis only updates the current editor state. You still need to tap Save to persist the workflow.

%1$d workflows still contain %2$d steps with %3$d enum parameters stored as legacy display values. Do you want to migrate them to the new version now?\n\nThis migration will immediately persist the affected workflows.
Backing up all workflows first is recommended. Do you want to export a backup before running the migration?\n\nSelecting "No" applies the migration immediately.
This screen shows locally saved crash history. Each crash also keeps one pending report so the app can surface it quickly on next launch.
Trigger a manual test crash, or wait for a real crash, and the local report will appear here.
This is the most recent crash report that has not been cleared yet.
After deletion, this local crash report will no longer be available inside the app.
This removes all archived crash reports and the pending crash report. This cannot be undone.
This immediately crashes the app so you can verify local crash capture and the next-launch recovery prompt.
The app will crash and exit immediately. Use this only to verify local crash logging.
A newer Core is bundled. Running: %1$s, bundled: %2$s. Tap to open Core Management and update.
Try a shorter keyword or a different phrase.
Try a shorter keyword or a different phrase.
Create a workflow or import an existing backup to get started.
Shows a permanent notification in the status bar when running in the background. Disabling this may cause the background service to be killed by the system, affecting trigger stability.
Restore it when the service stops unexpectedly
Automatically re-enables the accessibility service when it closes unexpectedly via Shizuku / Root. This feature requires Shizuku / Root permissions.
Filter variables and operators by type
When enabled, the variable picker filters variables by module input type, and IF/Loop module operators are also filtered by variable type. When disabled, it uses Shortcuts-style loose typing.
Allow Popups to Keep Screen On
Keep the screen awake while a popup is visible

When enabled, vFlow popups such as input prompts and error dialogs prevent the system from turning the screen off automatically while they are visible. Disabled by default to reduce battery drain from unattended popups.
Keep the CPU awake while a workflow is running

When enabled, vFlow automatically acquires a PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK while a workflow is running, and releases it as soon as the execution finishes, is cancelled, or fails. Disabled by default to reduce background battery drain.
When enabled, it will not appear in the recent tasks list.
For executing screenshots, Shell commands, and other operations
Keep logs in memory for debugging
Auto Enable Accessibility Service (Shizuku/Root not authorized)
Operation failed, please check Shell permissions
Diagnostic complete, please click "Export Logs" to view detailed results
Language changed, please restart app to apply
App restart needed to apply new language
UI Inspector started, drag icon to select controls
No workflow is available on the clipboard
Automatically removed %d unknown or deprecated modules.
AI has generated new workflow steps. Do you want to clear all current steps in the editor and apply the newly generated content?
Shizuku is not started, please start Shizuku service first
Overlay permission required to show quick control panel
To show quick control panels for favorite workflows, overlay permission is required.
A workflow named \'%s\' already exists (ID: %s...). How do you want to handle \'%s\' from the file?
\'%s\' has been imported as a copy
A module with ID \"%s\" already exists.\n\nDo you want to overwrite it?
Warning: %d workflow(s) are using this module:\n\n%sForcibly deleting will cause these workflows to malfunction.
Are you sure you want to delete module \"%s\"?
The app and Core watch each other and restart automatically if either process is killed
Connect to Core through a local UNIX socket instead of a TCP port. Takes effect after restarting Core.
UNIX socket setting saved. Restart Core to apply it.
Shizuku launch is unavailable when UNIX socket is enabled. Use Root instead.
Please enter API Key in settings
AI is generating, are you sure you want to interrupt?

Are you sure you want to download '%s'?

Description: %s

Version: v%s
Category: %s
Permissions: %s
A module with ID \'%s\' already exists. How do you want to handle \'%s\' from the repository?
[Content is too long and was truncated. Showing the first %1$d of %2$d characters]
Tap to start speech to text
Hold the button below to start speaking
Hold the button to record, release to stop.
Offline recognition mode: hold the button to record, release to stop.
Recognition complete. Hold again to continue or send now.
No speech recognized. Hold to try again.
Online recognition failed. Switched to offline recognition, please try again.
Speech recognition is not available on this device
Local Sherpa-ncnn model is not installed. Go to Settings -> Module Config -> Sherpa Speech Models to download or import a model, then try again.
Speech recognition is busy. Try again later.
The selected language does not have an offline speech pack installed. Download it in system voice settings and try again.
Gravity data not recorded yet. Re-record after enabling it.
Open the recorder, press any volume key once to start, and press a volume key again to stop.
Disabled by default. When enabled, recording and matching also include the 3-axis gravity vector, so re-recording is recommended.
Hold the device in the target pose, then press any volume key once to start sampling.
Keep the device steady and press a volume key again to finish and save the averaged pose.
Tip: higher thresholds are stricter. Around 90% fits stable poses, around 80% is better for handheld triggering.
This device does not support pose sensors
This device does not support gravity sensors
No pose data captured yet, please try again
No gravity data captured yet, please try again
Are you sure you want to download \'%s\'?\n\n%s
\'%s\' has been downloaded as a copy
A workflow named \'%s\' already exists (ID: %s...). How do you want to handle \'%s\' from the repository?
On error: Skip this step and continue
Metadata updated. Save the workflow to apply it.
When the workflow is triggered again
When the workflow is triggered again
Please enter a key name first
Only local files are supported. Please choose a file from local storage.
No workflow can handle this shared content
Error: workflow not found for ID\nID: %1$s
The current launcher does not support pinned shortcuts
Trigger matched, but execution was skipped due to missing permissions: %1$s
Workflow is already running, so this trigger was ignored
Current execution was stopped because the workflow was triggered again
Select a value for a specific key...
Select a value at a specific index...
Select a value at a specific index...
Please enter an API key in settings
AI is thinking and generating the workflow...
AI is generating. Do you want to interrupt it?
Use same choice for subsequent actions
This Activity is not exported, regular methods may not be able to start it
Select Image to Find on Screen
Not recorded\nClick button below to record touch operations
Search by app name or package
The shortcut picker needs Root or Shizuku to read system shortcut data
Describe your requirements, AI will automatically build the workflow for you.
Auto-infer type: true/false -> Boolean, number -> Integer/Float
Unable to open the image picker
Overlay permission is required to capture the screen
Shizuku or Root permission is required to capture the screen
Bluetooth permission is required to load the device list
This device does not support Bluetooth
Share mode: Variables are shared in the workflow call chain
Copy mode: Copy initial values from source workflow
No named variables in this workflow
This will delete every execution record shown in the recent logs sheet, and it cannot be undone.
Start Core using Shizuku permissions, no Root required
Unavailable when UNIX socket is enabled. Use Root launch instead.
Shizuku is not active or not authorized, cannot start
vFlow Core failed to start, please check logs
Start Core using Root permissions, requires rooted device
Device is not rooted or Root permission not granted, cannot start
Start Core from computer using ADB
Execute the following command on your computer:
• Ensure Android device is connected to computer via USB\n• USB debugging mode is enabled\n• ADB tools are installed on your computer
This will search for alive vFlow Core processes and kill them directly. Use this mainly to recover from address or port conflicts.
vFlow Core auto start status notifications
The saved startup mode (%1$s) is currently unavailable. Please start vFlow Core manually.
Please start vFlow Core manually once to record your startup preference.
Automatically start Core using the last used method when app starts or device reboots
Please manually start Core once to record your preference
Running: legacy Core without version info Bundled: %1$s
A newer bundled Core is available. Tap “Update Core” below to load it.
Required permissions are missing. Grant them from the main screen before running this workflow.
vFlow Core background service for performing advanced operations like simulated clicks and system actions. Can be started via Shizuku or Root.
Features that require vFlow Core to run with Root privileges.
Required for core automation features like clicks, find, and input.
Used to display Toast messages and send task result notifications.
Used for voice input features such as speech-to-text.
Allows the app to display input boxes and other windows while running in the background, essential for complex automation flows.
Allows the app to read and manipulate status bar notifications, used for notification triggers and notification management.
Allows the app to read and write scripts and resource files in /sdcard/vFlow directory.
Used to receive and read SMS messages to trigger workflows. Includes receive and read SMS permissions.
Used to control device Bluetooth on/off state.
Used for system-level settings like screen brightness adjustment.
Required on some Android versions to access saved Wi-Fi lists.
Required to continuously monitor location changes in the background, such as for location triggers.
Allows the app to perform elevated operations through Shizuku, such as Shell commands.
Add the app to battery optimization whitelist to ensure background triggers (like key listeners) run stably.
Used for "Time Trigger" feature to ensure workflows can be awakened and executed at precise times.
Allows the app to execute low-level system commands with superuser privileges.
Allows vFlow to read app usage statistics (like usage duration, last used time) for improved Agent accuracy.
Used to listen for phone state changes to implement the call trigger feature.
Allows the app to directly dial phone numbers.
Permission Guardian periodically checks key permissions and automatically re-grants them if revoked by the system. Requires Root or Shizuku permission.
Required for core automation features like tapping and finding elements
This feature requires Root or Shizuku permission
Are you sure you want to delete folder \"%s\"? Workflows in this folder will be kept and moved to the root directory.
Create your first workflow or import an existing backup.
vFlow is a powerful automation tool that helps you automatically perform repetitive mobile tasks and free your hands.
Configure Shizuku or Root permissions to unlock advanced features like simulating physical keys and background screenshots.
To simulate operations and perceive the screen, vFlow needs core permissions like accessibility.
We\'ve prepared a simple \"Hello World\" workflow for you. Tap start to begin your automation journey!
vFlow can use Shizuku or Root permissions to perform more powerful operations (such as simulating physical keys, background screenshots, etc.).
Use with Shizuku, requires Shizuku to be activated in advance.
Get the highest permission, still recommend activating Shizuku.
Only use accessibility service, some advanced features are unavailable.
Shizuku not running or not authorized
For smooth automation, vFlow needs the following core permissions.
If these required permissions are missing, some automation features may work incorrectly or not work at all. Do you still want to continue?
You have completed all basic configurations.\nWe have prepared a sample workflow for you,\nlet\'s experience automation now!
🎉 Congratulations! vFlow is configured successfully, your first workflow has been executed!
Cannot display floating window, please check permissions
Find device paths and key codes for physical keys
Click start button to begin listening
Shizuku/Root not available, cannot listen to keys

1. Enable the server switch 2. Visit the server URL to view API documentation 3. Generate a token for authentication 4. Add to request header: Authorization: Bearer <token>
Note: Ensure device and web editor are on the same network
Please save this token, it cannot be viewed again after closing
Failed to start, port may be in use
View server status and manage access tokens
Persistent global variables shared across workflows.
